17.4 No Disciplinary Action: Student with any pending indiscipline case not considered to have completed
requirements.
17.5 Award of Degrees: Provisional Certificate issued on Chairman Senate approval. Degree Certificate requires
Senate recommendation to Board of Governors and BoG approval in ensuing convocation.
17.6 Withdrawal of Degree: Senate may recommend BoG to withdraw a degree in case of gross violation of
graduation requirements detected at a later stage.

answer:
1. IIT Jodhpur Governance Structure (ASCII Tree)
2. The Senate of IIT Jodhpur reserves the right to modify/amend, without notice, Rules and Regulations of the Postgraduate programmes.
3. The Senate comprises:
    * Chairman, Board of Governors
    * Board of Governors (BoG)
    * Director
    * Senate
    * Institute
    * Buildings & Works Committee
    * Finance Committee
    * Dean of Faculty Affairs (DOFA)
    * Associate Dean, Faculty Affairs (ADFA)
    * Dean of Students Affairs (DOSA)
    * Associate Dean, Student Affairs (ADSA)
    * Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)
    * Associate Dean, Awards & Scholarships (ADAS)
    * Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)
    * Associate Dean, Awards & Scholarships (ADAS)
    * Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)
    * Associate Dean, Awards & Scholarships (ADAS)
    * Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)
    * Associate Dean, Awards & Scholarships (ADAS)
    * Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)
    * Associate Dean, Awards & Scholarships (ADAS)
    * Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)
    * Associate Dean, Awards & Scholarships (ADAS)
    * Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)
    * Associate Dean, Awards & Scholarships (ADAS)
    * Director
4.  The Senate oversees the governance structure of IIT Jodhpur.

[Reference 3 | Source: IITJ_Rules_Regulations_Structured.pdf | Page: 2]
The academic programmes are based on three tenets: (1) Semester System, (2) Credit System, and (3) Relative
Grading. These are administered by a duly constituted Academic Committee (AC) chaired by Dean (Academics).
The Senate formulates guidelines and rules for high standards and smooth functioning. Programmes can include
additional requirements subject to Senate approval.
2. ACADEMIC SESSION
The academic session runs from end of July in one year to mid-July of the next year, divided into:
[
  {
    "Term": "Semester I",
    "Period": "Fourth week of July to last week of November"
  },
  {
    "Term": "Semester II",
    "Period": "Last week of December to last week of April"
  },
  {
    "Term": "Summer Term",
    "Period": "Middle of May to middle of July"
  }
]
Excluding examination days, total instruction days per semester: at least 70.
2.1 Academic Calendar
The academic calendar specifies dates for orientation, registration, commencement of classes, add/drop of courses,
examinations, grade submission, project evaluation, results declaration, mid-semester recess, and vacation. Approved
by the Senate.
3. REGISTRATION

answer:
The B.Tech. Electrical Engineering program requires a total of 165 credits.

Here are some core courses:

*   Data Structures and Algorithms
*   Circuit Theory
*   Engineering Electromagnetics
*   Physical Electronics
*   VLSI Systems (EE and CS)
*   Smart Grid

Grade Descriptions:
A*: Same grade points as A. Awarded only in courses with 50+ registered students. Must not exceed 2% of strength.
Not awarded for projects/seminars.
E: Awarded when marks are below D cutoff but student has met 75% attendance. Student eligible for one additional
examination (within first week of next semester) to convert E to D.
I: Incomplete — awarded on student request due to absence on medical/special grounds. Must be resolved by last
date of add/drop of next semester.

CIRCULAR: Amendment in Attendance Policy — No.
IITJ/OAA/Circular/2024-25/51/68.2 — Dated: 08 July 2024
The Senate in its 38th Meeting (26th June 2024, resolution 38.2.7) approved an amendment to the existing attendance
policy.
Existing Policy: Student must have full attendance. Even below 75%, student allowed to appear for exams. Students
below 75% must score at least C grade; otherwise awarded F grade.
Revised Policy (supersedes earlier policy in totality):
Students expected to have full attendance unless leave is for valid medical or bonafide reasons. Attendance must not
fall below 75%. Attendance records made available weekly by instructors/TAs. Examination performance is NO
LONGER linked to course attendance.

---

[Reference 4 | Source: IITJ_Rules_Regulations_Structured.pdf | Page: 7]
Minor 1, Minor 2, and Major examinations are mandatory for regular 14-week lecture courses (Minors: 60 min; Major:
120 min). Fractal courses must have at least one examination. Total examination component weightage: 40%-60% of
total evaluation.
NOTE (2024 Amendment): Changed to one Minor and one Major examination (Minor: 2 hrs; Major: 3 hrs,
weightage >=40%). This new policy supersedes the earlier policy.
5. GRADING SYSTEM

*   Students are expected to have full attendance unless leave is for valid medical or bonafide reasons.
*   Attendance must not fall below 75%.
*   Attendance records are made available weekly by instructors/TAs.
*   Examination performance is NO LONGER linked to course attendance.
*   A new policy has been introduced that changed the existing policy.
*   Minor and Major examinations are mandatory for regular 14-week lecture courses (Minors: 60 min; Major: 120 min).
*   Fractal courses must have at least one examination.
*   Total examination component weightage: 40%-60% of total evaluation.

■ Statutory Bodies and Key Functionaries
Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur
1. IIT Jodhpur — Statutory Governance Structure
The governance hierarchy of IIT Jodhpur is structured as follows:
IIT Council
■■■ Chairman, Board of Governors
■■■ Board of Governors (BoG)
■■■ Director
■ ■■■ Senate
■ ■■■ Institute
■ ■■■ Buildings & Works Committee
■■■ Finance Committee

Director
■■■ Dean of Faculty Affairs (DOFA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Faculty Affairs (ADFA)
■■■ Dean of Students Affairs (DOSA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Student Affairs (ADSA)
■ ■■■ Associate Dean, Hostel Affairs (ADHA)

17.4 No Disciplinary Action: Student with any pending indiscipline case not considered to have completed
requirements.

Senate recommendation to Board of Governors and BoG approval in ensuing convocation.
17.6 Withdrawal of Degree: Senate may recommend BoG to withdraw a degree in case of gross violation of
graduation requirements detected at a later stage.

5.3.1 SGPA: Weighted average of grades in all courses credited in a semester (excluding audit and S/X graded
